description: Delivery-planning deliverable: turn three inputs (a data catalogue, a CSV source->target mapping, and a list of functional requirements) into a phased roadmap of user stories for an analytics build on Microsoft Fabric. Good output sequences the work ingestion -> transform -> model -> report across a medallion (bronze/silver/gold) Lakehouse, writes stories in 'As a / I want / so that' form with acceptance criteria, uses real Fabric components (Lakehouse/OneLake, Dataflow Gen2 or notebooks, Data Pipelines, Semantic model, Power BI), and traces stories back to the FRs. Deterministic floor checks story format, phasing, Fabric + medallion vocabulary, and acceptance criteria. Rubric judges sequencing, traceability and Fabric-appropriateness.
prompt:
You are planning an analytics build on Microsoft Fabric. Using the three inputs below, produce a delivery ROADMAP of USER STORIES.

Requirements for your answer:
- Organise the stories into delivery phases (or sprints), sequenced so each phase builds on the last (ingestion, then transformation/modelling, then reporting).
- Write each story in the form: \"As a <role>, I want <capability> so that <benefit>\", and give acceptance criteria for each.
- Map the work to Microsoft Fabric components (e.g. Lakehouse/OneLake, Data Pipelines, Dataflow Gen2 or notebooks, a semantic model, Power BI) and a medallion (bronze/silver/gold) architecture.
- Make the traceability explicit: each story should reference the functional requirement(s) it delivers.

--- INPUT 1: DATA CATALOGUE (available sources):
- pos_transactions   : receipt headers, one row per transaction (store_id, txn_ts, loyalty_id nullable, channel). ~3 yrs, daily file drop to ADLS.
- pos_lines          : receipt lines, one row per item (txn_id, sku, qty, gross_amount, discount_amount, return_flag).
- product_master     : sku, product_name, category, brand (weekly full extract).
- store_master       : store_id, store_name, region, format (SCD candidate — stores re-region occasionally).
- promotions         : promo_id, category, promo_type, start_date, end_date.
- loyalty_members    : loyalty_id, join_date, segment.

--- INPUT 2: CSV MAPPING (excerpt, source -> target dim_customer / fact_sales):
- pos_lines.qty          -> fact_sales.units
- pos_lines.gross_amount -> fact_sales.gross_amount
- pos_lines.discount_amount -> fact_sales.discount_amount
- pos_lines.return_flag  -> fact_sales.is_return
- store_master.region    -> dim_store.region
- product_master.category-> dim_product.category
- promotions.promo_id    -> dim_promotion.promotion_key (date-range join on category + txn date)

--- INPUT 3: FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS:
FR1. Report sales (units, gross, net) by store, region and product category over any user-selected date range.
FR2. Show returns separately from gross sales, with net = gross - returns.
FR3. Measure promotion effectiveness: compare category sales in a promotion window vs a comparable baseline period.
FR4. Segment all measures by loyalty member vs guest.
FR5. Support year-on-year and week-on-week comparison, including weeks that span a month boundary.
FR6. Refresh daily; retain three years of history.

checks.label: at least one full 'As a / I want / so that' story
traces stories back to the functional requirements

criteria: Phasing is logical and incremental: ingestion (bronze) -> cleanse/conform/model (silver/gold) -> semantic model + Power BI reporting, with each phase usable
Stories are well-formed (role/want/benefit), independently valuable, and carry meaningful acceptance criteria

Fabric architecture is appropriate and specific: Lakehouse/OneLake, Pipelines for orchestration/ingestion, Dataflow Gen2 or notebooks for transformation, star schema in gold, a semantic model, Power BI reports; daily refresh addressed
Traceability is real: every functional requirement (FR1-FR6) is covered by at least one story, and the store SCD / promotion date-range join from the mapping are handled
Realistic scope and ordering — foundational data plumbing before analytics, no story depending on something not yet built
